findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:155: "My brother Kal, his wife, and their two boys."
findings.problem: The Dao declaration contradicts the continuity sheet on a core kinship fact: the canon sheet identifies Kal as Hala's father, not her brother. Because this scene is the novel's formal public record of family structure, the mismatch reads as a continuity break rather than a character slip.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:153-156 so Hala names Kal in the canonical relationship established by the continuity sheet, and make the surrounding Dao roster in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:153-169 fully consistent with that family structure.
findings.acceptance_test: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:153-169, Kal's relationship to Hala and Bett matches the continuity sheet exactly, with no remaining kinship conflict when the declaration is read against the canonical family data.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:53 says, "The lamps stayed dark"; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:203 says Fasa "pinched out the one lamp she had lit."
findings.problem: The chapter loses control of a basic object state. If the hall's lamps stay dark through the naming, Fasa cannot extinguish a lamp the text never establishes as lit. That physical contradiction breaks the scene's authority.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either keep the hall unlit and change snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:203 so Fasa leaves without extinguishing a lamp, or add a clear on-page beat in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:195-203 showing her light a single lamp after the ceremony before she pinches it out.
findings.acceptance_test: Across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:53 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:195-203, the lamp state reads one way only: either no lamp is lit anywhere in this sequence, or one lamp is explicitly lit on-page before it is extinguished.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:9 pre-classifies the families before the ceremony; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:191-193 re-summarizes the same pressure points after the ceremony.
findings.problem: The chapter explains its own discoveries twice. Fasa labels several family tensions before the naming starts and then paraphrases them again after the scene, which reduces the ceremony's live voltage and lowers the sense of revelation the chapter needs at shortlist level.
findings.rewrite_direction: Trim or redistribute the interpretive summary in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:7-9 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:189-193. Keep only the judgments Fasa could not honestly make until the room was under pressure, and let the live exchanges in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:67-187 carry more of Jin's economy, Bett's refusal, the Leos' hesitation, and Gitta's lie.
findings.acceptance_test: After revision, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:7-9 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:189-193 do not pre-state and then restate the same family tells; at least three tensions named in those summary zones become legible first through the live ceremony in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:67-187.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:81, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:95, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:111, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:135, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:155, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:175 all open with near-identical roll-call declaration shapes.
findings.problem: The ritual constraint is real, but the chapter lets too many speakers collapse into the same "name-count-kin list" cadence. The style bible requires idiolect separation under pressure, and here several families momentarily sound like the ceremony's template instead of themselves.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the openings in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:69-175 so at least three families encode the required information through character-specific syntax, pressure, or omission rather than the same roll-call beat. Preserve the shrine protocol, but make Venn, Jin, Hala, and Cade distinguishable before Fasa starts probing.
findings.acceptance_test: In revised lines snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:69-175, no more than two consecutive family openings use the same declaration shape, and at least three openings are attributable by register alone when read without speaker tags.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:109-115 gives Jin clipped, high-pressure speech and then has him say, "We are going north."
findings.problem: Jin's route answer breaks his calibrated voice. The style bible sets him as a high-contraction, minimum-speech character whose public register is pared down and physical; the full-form "We are going north" sounds slightly ceremonial and writerly at the exact moment he should sound guarded.
findings.rewrite_direction: Adjust snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:115 so Jin answers in contracted or naturally fragmented speech that still preserves his evasiveness and minimalism.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised Jin exchange at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:109-127, his spoken lines contain no unexplained full-form auxiliaries, and the route answer at line 115 reads as clipped natural speech rather than formal declaration.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:107 ("The past tense landed harder"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:149 ("The sentence arrived clean"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:169 ("The words cost her"), and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:177 ("The word partner hit the room and stayed there") all render speech as a physical impact.
findings.problem: The chapter repeats the same speech-as-physical-impact construction four times in a short span. The first use works; by the fourth, it reads like a default explanatory tic and starts flattening the prose's pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:107, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:149, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:169, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:177 so no more than two of those beats rely on words landing, arriving, hitting, or otherwise becoming objects in the room. Let some pressure register through silence, body response, or spatial change instead.
findings.acceptance_test: Across revised lines snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:107-177, the prose uses at most two speech-impact constructions, and each cited beat creates pressure through a distinct mechanism rather than repeating the same formula.

summary: Controlled and often strong in Fasa's POV, but this chapter does not pass review. Two high-severity continuity/object-state contradictions damage the authority of the naming scene, and the ceremony's middle third becomes too patterned in exposition, dialogue shape, and sentence construction to clear shortlist-level standards.
